mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5% in 1 month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 215) triggers central bank rate hikes, creating a recession risk that drives negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215, or inflation expectations fail to translate into rate hikes within 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal stagflation, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through dual demand-supply shocks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls > 2% and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% monthly, or interaction effect size < 0.1 in rolling 5-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening (UNRATE < 4.5%) combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals corporate demand for labor, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS > 150k, or individual series effects reverse direction in sub-samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 115) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO rises > 1.5% monthly, or INDPRO's predictive power declines below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 1200) signal resilient consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through increased construction sector activity.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1200, or HOUST's correlation with SPX drops below 0.2 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1 < 0) signals economic recession, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through reduced corporate investment and consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 declines > 1% quarterly, or GDP's predictive power for SPX falls below 0.1 in rolling 5-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS > 380k) and housing starts (HOUST > 1200) signals robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where RSAFS and HOUST both rise > 2% monthly, or interaction effect size < 0.15 in rolling 5-year regressions.
